chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft

text: 5123 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ صَلَبَ رَجُلاً، بِالْحِيرَةِ ثَلاَثَةَ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ أَنْزَلَهُ يَوْمَ اَلرَّابِعِ فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ وَ دَفَنَهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5123 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), it is reported:
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) crucified a man in Al-Hirah for three days. Then on the fourth day, Imam (as) took him down, prayed over him, and buried him."
